summary:
In plasma, IDL undergoes hepatic clearance or converts into LDL for further processing; option C accurately reflects these two primary outcomes.
The agent explains how intermediate density lipoproteins (IDLs) get removed via direct liver absorption or transformed into low-density lipoproteins (LDL), which then gets managed by the liver cells. This process aligns with choice C as it captures all significant aspects regarding what happens to IDL within blood circulation. To condense this effectively:

In plasma, IDL faces removal by the liver or transforms into LDL for subsequent handling; thus, option C correctly summarizes possible fates of IDL.
